name: Hunyuan-A13B
archDisplay: Fine-grained sparse MoE Transformer, 1 shared + 64 routed experts, 8 active/token (80B total / 13B active)
note: Open-weights fine-grained MoE (80B total/13B active) with dual fast/slow reasoning, GQA and 256K context; leads open agentic benchmarks like BFCL-v3 (78.3).
tpsNote: 80B total params exceed a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM at 4-bit despite only 13B active; needs multi-GPU or CPU offload to serve.

results.notes: [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing 49.12 figure is pulled from the HF model card's separate 'Base Model' benchmark table (plain 'GPQA', pretrain checkpoint) -- confirmed present there via two independent fetches of the raw README. But the catalog entry represents the released Instruct/chat model (tencent/Hunyuan-A13B-Instruct, the same repo already cited for the other rows), and that model's own competitive-benchmark t]
Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard v3, agentic tool-use
Instruct-model competitive-table figure (base/pretrain table separately reports BBH=87.56, not used here to stay consistent with the Instruct framing of this catalog entry).

verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Tencent announced Hunyuan-A13B open-source release on 2025-06-27 (official Tencent Hunyuan X/Twitter post, GitHub repo, MarkTechPost coverage all corroborate). Fetched the Hugging Face model card directly and confirmed MMLU 88.17, AIME2024 87.3, MATH 94.3, GPQA 49.12 literally appear; fetched MarkTechPost article and confirmed BFCL-v3 78.3 literally appears. Confirmed 80B total/13B active MoE (1 shared + 64 routed experts, 8 active), 256K native context, and license identifier 'tencent-hunyuan-a13b' whose full text is the Tencent Hunyuan Community License Agreement (verified via GitHub LICENSE file).
